_meta.purpose: Per-state garage-door data dictionary for Phase A.2 state-vertical hub pages (16x7 single-bay or 18x7 double-bay residential garage door installation focus including opener replacement).
_meta.sources: ASCE 7-22 design wind speed maps; FBC §1620 / Miami-Dade HVHZ Notice of Acceptance (NOA) requirements; Florida Product Approval (FL #) database; ANSI/DASMA 108 wind-load test standard; ANSI/DASMA 115 high-velocity hurricane-zone test; IECC 2021 garage door R-value (Section R402.2.13); state contractor licensing boards verified by URL; BLS OEWS May 2024 (SOC 49-9031 Home Appliance Repairers + SOC 47-2031 Carpenters as garage-door installer wage anchors); Census 2020 American Community Survey housing data on garage prevalence by state; HOA prevalence data from Foundation for Community Association Research (FCAR); termite TPCT zone mapping for wood-door material decisions; UL 325 + UL 991 garage-door opener safety standards.

fields.design_wind_speed_mph: ASCE 7-22 mapped basic wind speed (Risk Category II, mph) — drives garage-door wind-pressure rating
fields.hurricane_tier: HVHZ / wind_high / wind_moderate / wind_low / none — coastal hurricane wind tier driving DASMA 115 + Miami-Dade NOA requirements
fields.dominant_door_material: steel-insulated / aluminum-glass / wood-composite / wood-solid / fiberglass / vinyl
fields.dominant_opener_type: belt-drive / chain-drive / direct-drive / jackshaft-side-mount / screw-drive — dominant residential opener selection by region
fields.iecc_zone: IECC 2021 climate zone — drives R-value mandate per R402.2.13
fields.garage_door_r_value_required: R-value mandate for new-construction garage door per IECC 2021 amendments (e.g., R-13, R-16, R-19, or none)
fields.frost_line_inches: USDA-NRCS design frost depth (inches) — drives garage-floor concrete pour spec but not door spec
fields.termite_risk: very-high / high / moderate / low — TPCT zone for wood-jamb framing decisions
fields.hoa_prevalence: very-high / high / moderate / low — HOA-driven color/material/style restrictions
fields.salt_air_corrosion: high-coastal / moderate-coastal / low / none — drives stainless hardware + zinc-coated track requirement
fields.license_board_name: Issuing authority for residential garage-door contractor licensing
fields.permit_typical: Typical permit threshold for residential garage-door replacement
fields.garage_door_installer_wage_mean_hourly: BLS OEWS May 2024 state mean hourly wage anchor for garage-door installer (SOC 49-9031 Home Appliance Repairer / SOC 47-2031 Carpenter blend) (USD)
fields.avg_install_low: Typical low end of 16x7 single-bay or 18x7 double-bay steel-insulated garage door + opener replacement (USD)
fields.avg_install_high: Typical high end (HVHZ-NOA-rated impact-resistant door + jackshaft opener + custom-color carriage-house style) (USD)
fields.distinctive_law: Single most distinctive state-specific garage-door wind-rating, HOA, license, or building-code rule
fields.climate_concern: Primary climate, wind, salt-air, termite, or HOA driver of garage-door material and rating selection in this state

AL.name: Alabama
AL.license_board_name: Alabama Home Builders Licensure Board (HBLB) — Limited Specialty Construction credential for garage-door installation
AL.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening on new construction; HBLB residential builder license required above $10,000 contract; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt below $10,000
AL.distinctive_law: Alabama Code §34-14A makes the Alabama Home Builders Licensure Board the only state body that licenses residential garage-door contractors above the $10,000 threshold under a Limited Specialty Construction credential, plus Alabama is one of the only Gulf states requiring DASMA 108 wind-load testing on every Mobile-Baldwin coastal-county garage-door pour to address Gulf hurricane wind exposure with continuous tie-down detailing from header to slab on every coastal residential garage door.
AL.climate_concern: Alabama's combination of Gulf hurricane wind exposure across Mobile and Baldwin counties driving 130 mph design wind requirements plus DASMA 108 wind-load test compliance on every coastal residential garage-door installation, very-high TPCT termite zone 1 pressure across the entire state driving pressure-treated wood jamb framing on every garage-door installation, and persistent Gulf Coast humidity driving rapid corrosion on standard galvanized springs and bottom seals on every coastal garage-door retrofit drives unique salt-air-and-termite-driven garage-door material selection.

AK.name: Alaska
AK.license_board_name: Alaska Department of Commerce — Construction Contractors Section (Specialty: Residential Endorsement)
AK.permit_typical: borough building permit required for new garage-door opening; Construction Contractor with Residential Endorsement required; mandatory R-19 insulated-door specification on every Anchorage and Fairbanks residential install per Alaska state energy code amendments
AK.distinctive_law: Alaska Statute §18.65.082 requires every Alaska garage-door contractor to hold a Construction Contractor with Residential Endorsement plus carry a $20,000 surety bond, and the Alaska state energy code amendments separately mandate R-19 insulated garage-door specification on every Anchorage, Fairbanks, Juneau, and Mat-Su Borough residential installation — the highest residential garage-door R-value mandate in the country to address -50°F interior winter low temperatures with attached garages contributing significant heat loss to the heated building envelope.
AK.climate_concern: Alaska's combination of -50°F interior winter low temperatures across Fairbanks, North Pole, and Delta Junction driving R-19 maximum-insulation garage-door specification on every residential install, persistent Pacific salt-spray on every Anchorage, Juneau, Sitka, Ketchikan, and Kodiak coastal exposure driving stainless-hardware-and-zinc-coated-track replacement at 7-10 year intervals, and tropical-style Aleutian Chain hurricane wind exposure forcing 100 mph design wind requirements on every Aleutian and Bristol Bay residential garage-door installation drives extreme cold-climate-and-corrosion-driven material selection.

AZ.name: Arizona
AZ.license_board_name: Arizona Registrar of Contractors — license CR-9 (Concrete) or CR-31 (Carpentry) commonly held by garage-door installers; specialty CR-65 Garage Doors available
AZ.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening on new construction; ROC specialty license required on contracts above $1,000; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; HOA architectural committee approval mandatory in 80%+ of Phoenix-area subdivisions
AZ.distinctive_law: Arizona Registrar of Contractors §32-1121 sets the contracting threshold at $1,000 with ROC specialty CR-65 Garage Doors available as a dedicated credential, and Arizona is unusual among Mountain West states in HOA architectural-control prevalence — the Foundation for Community Association Research documents 80%+ of Phoenix, Tucson, Mesa, Chandler, Gilbert, and Scottsdale residential subdivisions operate HOA architectural committees with mandatory pre-approval review on garage-door color, material, and style selection plus mandatory Sun-Belt-rated UV-stabilized topcoats on every west-facing garage-door installation.
AZ.climate_concern: Arizona's combination of extreme summer UV exposure across the Phoenix and Tucson basins driving Sun-Belt-rated UV-stabilized polyester topcoats on every west-facing garage-door installation to prevent fade in 5-7 years, very-high TPCT termite zone 1 pressure driving pressure-treated wood jamb framing on every garage-door installation, and HOA architectural-control prevalence at 80%+ of Phoenix, Tucson, Mesa, and Scottsdale residential subdivisions requiring pre-approval review on door color, material, and style drives unique HOA-and-UV-driven material selection.

AR.name: Arkansas
AR.license_board_name: Arkansas Contractors Licensing Board — Residential Builder license (>$2,000)
AR.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; Residential Builder license required above $2,000 contract; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; tornado-shelter rough-in mandatory under Little Rock, Conway, and Fayetteville municipal codes
AR.distinctive_law: Arkansas Code §17-25-101 sets one of the lowest residential contracting thresholds at $2,000 with the Arkansas Contractors Licensing Board issuing a Residential Builder license, and Arkansas is unusual among Tornado Alley states in not enforcing a statewide tornado-shelter mandate although individual Little Rock, Conway, Fayetteville, and Bentonville municipalities separately require tornado-shelter rough-in detailing within attached garages on every residential foundation pour, with door-and-track impact-resistance rating recommended though not mandated.
AR.climate_concern: Arkansas's Tornado Alley exposure drives optional FEMA P-320 storm-shelter rough-in detailing within attached garages on every residential foundation pour to provide above-ground refuge during EF3+ tornado events, with Arkansas Department of Emergency Management documenting an average 30 tornadoes per year, and high TPCT termite zone 2 pressure across the southern half driving pressure-treated wood jamb framing on every Little Rock, Pine Bluff, El Dorado, and Texarkana residential garage-door installation.

CA.name: California
CA.license_board_name: California Contractors State License Board (CSLB) — D-28 Doors, Gates & Activating Devices specialty
CA.permit_typical: city building permit required for new garage-door opening on new construction; CSLB D-28 specialty license required above $500 contract; replacement-only installs require permit if structural modification involved; HOA architectural committee approval mandatory in 60%+ of California subdivisions; CALGreen Title 24 Part 11 mandatory recycled-content review on every door installation
CA.distinctive_law: California Contractors State License Board (CSLB) §7058 imposes one of the most credential-segmented residential contractor licensing schemes in the country with a dedicated D-28 Doors, Gates & Activating Devices specialty classification covering garage-door installation, gate-operator installation, and access-control system installation, and CALGreen Title 24 Part 11 separately requires recycled-content review on every garage-door installation specifying the source-reduction or recycled-content percentage of the steel skin, plus California Energy Code Title 24 Part 6 mandates UL-listed photo-eye reversing-edge sensors and listing on every UL 325-compliant residential opener installed in California.
CA.climate_concern: California's combination of Pacific salt-spray on every coastal exposure from Crescent City to San Diego driving stainless-hardware-and-zinc-coated-track replacement at 7-10 year intervals on every coastal residential garage-door installation, CAL FIRE Tier 3 WUI overlay across the Sierra foothills additionally requiring Class A fire-rated garage-door specification on every WUI residential property, very-high HOA architectural-control prevalence across 60%+ of California subdivisions requiring pre-approval review on door color/material/style, and high TPCT termite zone 2 pressure across central and southern California drives unique fire-and-corrosion-and-HOA-driven garage-door material selection.

CO.name: Colorado
CO.license_board_name: No statewide garage-door license — Denver, Colorado Springs, Aurora, and other home-rule municipalities issue local Building Contractor credentials
CO.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; no state license required; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; HOA architectural committee approval mandatory in 70%+ of Front Range subdivisions; Colorado state energy code amendments mandate R-16 minimum garage-door insulation
CO.distinctive_law: Colorado Revised Statutes §38-33.3 (Colorado Common Interest Ownership Act) provides one of the most enforceable HOA architectural-control statutes in the country with mandatory pre-approval review on garage-door color/material/style across 70%+ of Front Range subdivisions in Aurora, Castle Rock, Highlands Ranch, Parker, Centennial, and Lone Tree, and Colorado state energy code amendments separately mandate R-16 minimum garage-door insulation on every new-construction residential install — one of the highest mandatory R-value tiers below Alaska and Minnesota's R-19.
CO.climate_concern: Colorado's combination of high-elevation freeze-thaw cycling across the Front Range driving sealant degradation on every garage-door bottom seal at 4-6 year intervals, road de-icing salt exposure on every grade-level garage-door bottom edge driving stainless-hardware-and-zinc-coated-track requirement on every Front Range install, and high HOA architectural-control prevalence across 70%+ of Aurora, Castle Rock, Highlands Ranch, and Parker residential subdivisions plus Colorado state energy code R-16 minimum mandate drives unique cold-climate-and-HOA-driven garage-door specification.

CT.name: Connecticut
CT.license_board_name: Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection — Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) registration
CT.permit_typical: city or town building permit required for new garage-door opening; HIC registration required above $200 contract; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; coastal Atlantic counties mandate DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating on every garage-door installation
CT.distinctive_law: Connecticut General Statutes §20-419 creates the lowest-threshold mandatory Home Improvement Contractor registration in the country at $200 contract value covering every residential garage-door installation, with the DCP Guaranty Fund compensating homeowners up to $25,000 per project for HIC-contractor abandonment, and Connecticut Atlantic coastal counties (Fairfield, New Haven, Middlesex, New London) separately mandate DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating on every garage-door installation to address Long Island Sound hurricane exposure with 130 mph ASCE 7-22 design wind speed.
CT.climate_concern: Connecticut's combination of 130 mph ASCE 7-22 design wind speed across Atlantic coastal counties driving DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating on every coastal residential garage-door installation, persistent Long Island Sound salt-spray on every Fairfield, New Haven, Middlesex, and New London county exposure driving stainless-hardware replacement, F2 freeze-thaw cycling driving sealant degradation on every garage-door bottom seal at 4-6 year intervals, and 42-inch frost depth drives aggressive coastal-wind-and-cold-climate-driven material selection.

DE.name: Delaware
DE.license_board_name: Delaware Division of Revenue — Business License (garage-door installer)
DE.permit_typical: county building permit required for new garage-door opening; Delaware Business License only — no state-issued specialty license; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; Sussex County coastal counties mandate DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating
DE.distinctive_law: Delaware is one of the few East Coast states with no specialty garage-door contractor licensing — every Delaware garage-door contractor operates under a Delaware Division of Revenue Business License only, with no examination, bond, or board oversight, leaving Sussex County coastal property owners to rely on contractor BBB ratings rather than a state credential check, and Delaware Sussex County coastal jurisdictions separately require DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating on every garage-door installation to address Atlantic and Delaware Bay hurricane exposure with 130 mph ASCE 7-22 design wind speed.
DE.climate_concern: Delaware's combination of 130 mph ASCE 7-22 design wind speed across Sussex County and southern Kent County driving DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating on every coastal residential garage-door installation, persistent Atlantic and Delaware Bay salt-spray on every Sussex County coastal exposure driving stainless-hardware-and-zinc-coated-track requirement, and FEMA NFIP base-flood-elevation foundation height verification on every Bethany Beach to Rehoboth coastal residential foundation drives unique coastal-wind-and-corrosion-driven garage-door material selection.

FL.name: Florida
FL.license_board_name: Florida DBPR Construction Industry Licensing Board — Specialty Garage Door Contractor (CV-C-1518) or Certified Building Contractor
FL.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for every garage-door installation in Miami-Dade and Broward counties (HVHZ); Florida Product Approval (FL #) NOA verification mandatory on every HVHZ install; FL DBPR Specialty Garage Door Contractor license required statewide
FL.distinctive_law: Florida Building Code §1620 + ANSI/DASMA 115 imposes the most restrictive coastal hurricane-zone garage-door requirements in the country — every HVHZ residential garage-door installation in Miami-Dade and Broward counties requires Florida Product Approval (FL #) compliance with Notice of Acceptance (NOA) testing for hurricane wind uplift up to 175 mph plus large-missile impact resistance per ASTM E1996 (D-rated), and FL DBPR separately licenses a Specialty Garage Door Contractor (license code CV-C-1518) with mandatory examination on Florida Building Code wind-pressure calculations, FBC 1626 large-missile impact testing, and HVHZ NOA submittal documentation — the only dedicated state-level garage-door specialty license in the U.S.
FL.climate_concern: Florida's HVHZ Miami-Dade and Broward 175 mph design wind speed plus large-missile impact resistance requirement under FBC §1620 + ANSI/DASMA 115 drives the most expensive residential garage-door installation requirements in the country at $4,000-$8,000 for a Miami-Dade NOA-rated D-rated impact-resistant residential garage door, plus persistent Atlantic and Gulf salt-spray on every Florida coastal exposure driving stainless-hardware-and-zinc-coated-track replacement at 5-8 year intervals, very-high TPCT termite zone 1 pressure forcing pressure-treated wood jamb framing, and very-high HOA architectural-control prevalence across 80%+ of Florida subdivisions drives unique HVHZ-and-coastal-driven material selection.

GA.name: Georgia
GA.license_board_name: Georgia State Licensing Board for Residential and General Contractors — residential-light or residential-basic
GA.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; residential-light or residential-basic contractor license required above $2,500 contract; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; Georgia coastal Atlantic counties mandate DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating
GA.distinctive_law: Georgia Code §43-41 created the State Licensing Board for Residential and General Contractors in 2008 with the residential-light contractor license dedicated to projects under 5,000 sq ft and the residential-basic license covering full new-construction work — every Georgia garage-door contractor exceeding the $2,500 contract threshold must hold one of these credentials, plus Georgia Code §44-3 separately enforces the Georgia Property Owners Association Act requiring HOA architectural-committee pre-approval on every Atlanta-metro garage-door installation in deed-restricted communities.
GA.climate_concern: Georgia's combination of Atlantic hurricane wind exposure across coastal counties (Chatham, Bryan, Liberty, McIntosh, Glynn, Camden) driving DASMA 108 wind-pressure rating on every coastal residential garage-door installation, very-high TPCT termite zone 1 pressure across the entire state driving pressure-treated wood jamb framing on every Atlanta, Athens, Macon, Augusta, and Savannah residential install, persistent Gulf-Atlantic humidity driving rapid corrosion on standard galvanized springs at 5-7 year intervals, and high HOA architectural-control prevalence across Atlanta-metro subdivisions drives unique hurricane-and-termite-driven material selection.

HI.name: Hawaii
HI.license_board_name: Hawaii Department of Commerce & Consumer Affairs — Contractors License Board (C-31 Cement Concrete or C-22b Building Trades / Carpentry)
HI.permit_typical: county building permit required for new garage-door opening; HI DCCA C-22b license required statewide; mandatory hurricane-rated post-Iniki tie-down detailing on every Kauai residential garage-door installation; volcanic-ash-fall load review on every Big Island Hilo and Kona residential install
HI.distinctive_law: Hawaii Revised Statutes §444 imposes one of the most credential-segmented residential contractor licensing schemes in the country — every Hawaii garage-door contractor must hold a HI DCCA C-22b Building Trades / Carpentry specialty license, and Hawaii County (Big Island) Building Division separately requires volcanic-ash-fall load review on every residential garage-door installation in Mauna Loa and Kilauea zone-1 lava-flow areas plus Hurricane Iniki-revised tie-down requirements on every Kauai residential garage-door installation since 1992 to address Pacific hurricane wind exposure with 130 mph ASCE 7-22 design wind speed.
HI.climate_concern: Hawaii's combination of persistent Pacific salt-spray on every coastal exposure driving stainless-hardware-and-zinc-coated-track replacement at 5-7 year intervals on every Hawaii residential garage-door installation, tropical hurricane wind exposure driving Hurricane-Iniki-revised tie-down requirements on every Kauai residential garage-door installation, very-high TPCT termite zone 1 pressure across all islands driving pressure-treated wood jamb framing, and Big Island volcanic-ash-fall load review on every Mauna Loa and Kilauea zone-1 lava-flow residential install drives unique tropical-corrosion-and-volcanic-driven material selection.

ID.name: Idaho
ID.license_board_name: Idaho Bureau of Occupational Licenses — Public Works Contractor License (PWCL) for residential >$2,000 + Idaho Contractors Registration
ID.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; PWCL required for residential work above $2,000; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; Treasure Valley HOA architectural-committee approval common on Boise-Meridian-Nampa subdivisions
ID.distinctive_law: Idaho is one of only a few Mountain West states without a centralized residential builder license — Idaho Bureau of Occupational Licenses operates a Public Works Contractor License (PWCL) for residential garage-door work above $2,000 plus a Contractors Registration system without examination requirement, leaving Treasure Valley homeowners to rely on PWCL bond (only $25,000) and Idaho Contractor Registration history rather than a state-board credential check, with Boise, Meridian, and Nampa HOA architectural committees separately enforcing pre-approval review on door color, material, and style.
ID.climate_concern: Idaho's combination of 36-inch frost depth across the Treasure Valley driving sealant degradation on every garage-door bottom seal at 4-6 year intervals, statewide F2 freeze-thaw cycling, and Idaho northern panhandle alpine elevations above 4,000 feet driving R-16 minimum insulated-door specification on every Coeur d'Alene, Sandpoint, and Bonners Ferry residential install drives moderate cold-climate-driven garage-door material selection without the salt-air or hurricane drivers of coastal states.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.license_board_name: No statewide garage-door license — Cook County, City of Chicago, and most home-rule municipalities issue Building Contractor licenses
IL.permit_typical: city building permit required for new garage-door opening on new construction; Cook County and Chicago municipal licenses required; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; tornado-shelter rough-in within attached garage common in southern Illinois municipalities
IL.distinctive_law: Illinois is one of the few Midwest states without a statewide residential builder license — Cook County, City of Chicago, and most home-rule municipalities issue municipal Building Contractor licenses with Chicago specifically requiring a separate Class B General Contractor license with bond and examination, leaving Cook, Lake, McHenry, DuPage, and Will county homeowners to rely on municipal license history rather than a state credential check, plus southern Illinois municipalities separately recommend tornado-shelter rough-in detailing within attached garages on every residential foundation pour to provide above-ground refuge during EF3+ tornado events.

IN.name: Indiana
IN.license_board_name: No statewide garage-door license — Marion County (Indianapolis), Hamilton County, and other home-rule municipalities issue local Builder Contractor credentials
IN.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; municipal license required; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; tornado-shelter rough-in common across southern Indiana municipalities
IN.distinctive_law: Indiana Code §22-12 leaves residential garage-door licensing entirely to municipal Building Departments with Marion County, Hamilton County, Allen County, and Lake County issuing local Builder Contractor credentials but no centralized state board oversees garage-door contractor competency or bond requirements, leaving Indianapolis-area homeowners to rely on municipal license history rather than a state-board examination check, plus southern Indiana Tornado Alley exposure drives optional FEMA P-320 storm-shelter rough-in detailing within attached garages on every Bloomington, Evansville, and Terre Haute residential install.

IA.name: Iowa
IA.license_board_name: Iowa Department of Workforce Development — Iowa Construction Contractor Registration
IA.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; Iowa Construction Contractor Registration required for any contract above $2,000; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; tornado-shelter rough-in within attached garage common in Iowa Tornado Alley municipalities
IA.distinctive_law: Iowa Code §91C requires every Iowa garage-door contractor to register with the Iowa Construction Contractor Registration program plus carry a $25,000 surety bond — making Iowa one of the few states without a centralized residential builder license but with a meaningful financial-security requirement, plus Iowa Department of Public Safety and the Iowa Storm Shelter Initiative separately recommend FEMA P-320 storm-shelter rough-in detailing within attached garages on every residential foundation pour due to Iowa Tornado Alley exposure with the Iowa Severe Weather Awareness program documenting an average 51 tornadoes per year.

KS.name: Kansas
KS.license_board_name: Kansas Real Estate Commission Builder Disclosure + Local Building Department — no state garage-door contractor license
KS.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; no statewide builder license required; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; storm-shelter rough-in within attached garage commonly required under KS Building Code §1907 in tornado-prone counties
KS.distinctive_law: Kansas has no statewide residential garage-door contractor license — local building departments issue municipal contractor credentials in Wichita, Overland Park, Kansas City, Topeka, and Lawrence, but no centralized state board oversees garage-door contractor competency or bond requirements, leaving Kansas homeowners to rely on municipal license history rather than a state-board examination check, with individual Wichita, Overland Park, and Topeka municipalities separately recommending FEMA P-320 storm-shelter rough-in detailing within attached garages on every residential foundation pour due to Kansas Tornado Alley exposure with NWS Topeka documenting an average 96 tornadoes per year.

KY.name: Kentucky
KY.license_board_name: Kentucky Department of Housing, Buildings & Construction — Builder License for residential general contractors above 1,500 sq ft new construction
KY.permit_typical: city or county building permit required for new garage-door opening; KY DHBC Builder License required for new construction above 1,500 sq ft; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt; western KY counties (within New Madrid Seismic Zone) require seismic-detailed garage-door header attachment
KY.distinctive_law: Kentucky Department of Housing, Buildings & Construction §198B operates a Builder License only for residential general contractors performing new-construction work above 1,500 sq ft footprint, leaving garage-door replacement-only contractors entirely outside state credential review with all oversight falling to local building departments, plus Kentucky New Madrid Seismic Zone influence across western Kentucky counties (Hickman, Fulton, Carlisle, Ballard, McCracken, Graves) forces ASCE 7-22 SDC C-D0 seismic-detailed garage-door header attachment on every western Kentucky residential garage-door installation.

LA.license_board_name: Louisiana State Licensing Board for Contractors — Residential Building Contractor + Specialty Garage Door
LA.permit_typical: city or parish building permit required for new garage-door opening; LSLBC Residential Building Contractor license required above $75,000; Louisiana Uniform Construction Code (LUCC) hurricane wind tie-down mandatory on every coastal-parish garage-door installation; FEMA NFIP base-flood-elevation foundation height required on every coastal-parish residential install

ME.name: Maine
ME.license_board_name: Maine has no statewide residential builder license — Maine Office of Professional & Financial Regulation oversees specialty trades only
ME.permit_typical: town or municipal building permit required for new garage-door opening; no state license; Maine MUBEC adoption mandates IRC + IECC compliance with Maine state energy code amendments requiring R-16 minimum garage-door insulation; replacement-only installs typically permit-exempt
ME.distinctive_law: Maine has no statewide residential builder license — Maine Office of Professional & Financial Regulation oversees only specialty trades like electricians, plumbers, and oil-burner installers, leaving garage-door contractor competency and bond entirely to municipal building departments and homeowner due diligence, plus Maine Uniform Building & Energy Code (MUBEC) since 2010 separately mandates R-16 minimum garage-door insulation on every Maine residential installation as one of the highest mandatory R-value tiers in the U.S. Northeast to address 60-inch frost depth across Aroostook County and -30°F winter low temperatures.

MD.name: Maryland
MD.license_board_name: Maryland Home Improvement Commission (MHIC) — Home Improvement Contractor license + Maryland Department of Labor New Home Construction Sub-Contractor
